from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase (more concise for common predicates)
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "was started in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is in",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "leader is",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to avoid repeating the subject in every clause.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        # Aggregate objects per predicate
        pred_objs = {}
        for tr in group:
            pred_objs.setdefault(tr.predicate, []).append(tr.object)

        # Helper to clean and deduplicate objects
        def clean_objs(objs):
            cleaned = [o.strip('"').strip("'") for o in objs]
            # preserve order, remove exact duplicates
            seen = set()
            uniq = []
            for c in cleaned:
                if c not in seen:
                    seen.add(c)
                    uniq.append(c)
            return uniq

        # Helper to join a list with commas and "and"
        def join_list(items):
            if not items:
                return ""
            if len(items) == 1:
                return items[0]
            return ", ".join(items[:-1]) + " and " + items[-1]

        parts = []

        # Define ordering of predicates for more natural flow
        ordering = [
            "location", "country", "region", "state", "city", "capital",
            "leader", "leaderTitle", "ethnicGroup", "language", "currency",
            "architect", "owner", "developer", "tenant", "currentTenants",
            "buildingStartDate", "inaugurationDate", "completionDate",
            "floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms", "height",
            "floorArea", "cost", "buildingType", "formerName", "address",
            "website", "yearOfConstruction", "significantBuilding",
            "significantProject", "origin", "foundationPlace", "demonym",
            "postalCode", "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber",
            "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces", "keyPerson",
            "architecture"
        ]

        # Process predicates according to ordering
        for pred in ordering:
            if pred not in pred_objs:
                continue
            objs = clean_objs(pred_objs[pred])

            # Special handling for some predicates
            if pred == "leader":
                objs_str = join_list(objs)
                clause = f"{objs_str} {'are' if len(objs) > 1 else 'is'} {'leaders' if len(objs) > 1 else 'leader'} of {subject}"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "owner":
                objs_str = join_list(objs)
                clause = f"{objs_str} {'own' if len(objs) > 1 else 'owns'} {subject}"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "architect":
                objs_str = join_list(objs)
                clause = f"{objs_str} {'were' if len(objs) > 1 else 'was'} the architect{'s' if len(objs) > 1 else ''} of {subject}"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "tenant":
                objs_str = join_list(objs)
                clause = f"{objs_str} {'are' if len(objs) > 1 else 'is'} tenant{'s' if len(objs) > 1 else ''} of {subject}"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "currentTenants":
                objs_str = join_list(objs)
                clause = f"{subject} currently houses {objs_str}"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "ethnicGroup":
                objs_str = join_list(objs)
                clause = f"{subject} has ethnic group(s) including {objs_str}"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "capital":
                objs_str = join_list(objs)
                clause = f"The capital of {subject} is {objs_str}"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "language":
                objs_str = join_list(objs)
                clause = f"The language(s) of {subject} { 'are' if len(objs)>1 else 'is' } {objs_str}"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "demonym":
                objs_str = join_list(objs)
                clause = f"The demonym of {subject} is {objs_str}"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "buildingStartDate":
                clause = f"Construction of {subject} began in {join_list(objs)}"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "inaugurationDate":
                clause = f"The inauguration of {subject} was on {join_list(objs)}"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "completionDate":
                clause = f"{subject} was completed in {join_list(objs)}"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "floorCount":
                clause = f"{subject} has {join_list(objs)} floors"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "bedCount":
                clause = f"{subject} has {join_list(objs)} beds"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "numberOfRooms":
                clause = f"{subject} has {join_list(objs)} rooms"
                parts.append(clause)
            elif pred == "height":
                clause = f"{subject} rises to {join_list(objs)} metres"
                parts.append(clause)
            else:
                # Generic handling using verb_map
                verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
                objs_str = join_list(objs)
                clause = f"{subject} {verb} {objs_str}"
                parts.append(clause)

        # Build sentence for this subject
        if parts:
            sentence = ", ".join(parts) + "."
        else:
            sentence = f"{subject}."

        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine all subject sentences into one paragraph
    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    # Capitalize first character
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
